dependencies: Reliance on contract manufacturers for manufacturing needs
Use of variety of independent third-party companies for printed-circuit board assembly, in-circuit test, product repair, and product assembly
Dependence on sole suppliers for certain components
Limited number of contract manufacturers and suppliers
Supply chain concentration in Asia, especially China
risks: Financial problems of contract manufacturers or component suppliers
Reservation of manufacturing capacity by other companies
Industry consolidation within component supplier markets (semiconductor market)
Supply chain disruptions from public health emergencies, geopolitical tensions (China-Taiwan relations), natural disasters, climate change
Component shortages affecting lead times and ability to meet customer demand
Single-source or limited-source availability for certain components
Supplier constraints based on labor and other actions during economic downturns
Vendor allocation of products to certain customers for business, regulatory or political reasons
Demand changes in agreed pricing as condition of supply
Capacity problems at suppliers or contract manufacturers
Global supply chain disruptions affecting flow of components and product delivery

regulatory_risks: Privacy, data protection and personal information regulations (EU GDPR, California Consumer Privacy Act, Singapore Personal Data Protection Act)
Cybersecurity regulations and operational resilience requirements
Artificial intelligence regulations (proposed EU AI legislation)
Export and import control requirements and changes
Economic sanctions impacting ability to sell and support products in certain jurisdictions
Telecommunications regulations affecting service provider customers
Encryption technology regulations
Environmental sustainability regulations including climate change laws
Product certification requirements
Product accessibility requirements
Country of origin requirements
National security controls applicable to supply chain
Government procurement policies, priorities, regulations, and technology initiatives
Tax laws and regulations changes (Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, OECD Pillar Two framework imposing 15% minimum tax rate)
Transfer pricing regulations
Foreign-derived intangible income, global intangible low-tax income, base erosion and anti-abuse tax regulations
Research and development capitalization and amortization requirements
Corporate alternative minimum tax
Human rights regulations
Trade protection measures including tariffs
Withholding taxes on global operations
Environmental protection regulations

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions and trade restrictions related to Russia-Ukraine war
Sanctions and restrictions impacting ability to sell products in certain jurisdictions
Political relationship tensions between United States and other countries affecting regulatory matters and customer willingness to purchase from US companies
Access to components potentially denied by government bodies
Intelligence gathering concerns affecting customer trust in US IT suppliers
